channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: PM Shehbaz Unveils Pakistan's Largest Data Center and AI Cloud in Islamabad
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif inaugurated the Sky47 AI cloud and data centre in Islamabad, marking a significant step in Pakistan's technological advancement. The facility, described as the country's ...

item.title: 18-year-old woman's murder in UP's Shamli linked to parents' opposition to her relationship
item.description: An 18-year-old woman, Hina, was allegedly murdered by her parents in Shamli district of Uttar Pradesh over their opposition to her relationship with a youth. The police, based on a complaint from her ...

item.title: Labor avoids gas tax commitment amid One Nation pressure and public demand for industry reform
item.description: The article discusses the Australian Labor Party's decision to abandon plans to enshrine a gas tax in its platform during its national conference. Despite initial hopes to address calls for a fairer r...

item.title: The Strokes release documentary on new album 'Reality Awaits'
item.description: The Strokes have released 'In Transit: Part II', a 10-minute documentary offering a behind-the-scenes look at the creation of their seventh album 'Reality Awaits'. Directed by Johann Rashid, the film ...

item.title: Google Introduces Selfie Video Login Feature for Enhanced Security
item.description: Google has launched a new login feature allowing users to authenticate their accounts using selfie videos. This option adds another layer of biometric verification, complementing existing methods like...

item.title: Convicted Killer Receives Sentence for Second B.C. Murder
item.description: A convicted murderer has been sentenced for a second killing in British Columbia, marking the latest development in a case that has drawn significant attention to the province's criminal justice syste...

item.title: Labor supports telehealth in voluntary assisted dying care
item.description: At the Australian Labor Party's national conference, members voted to include support for telehealth in voluntary assisted dying care within the party's platform. The amendment, passed via voice vote,...

item.title: Tropical Storm Kiyapo lands in Camiguin Island with strong winds and heavy rains
item.description: Tropical Storm Kiyapo made landfall on July 24, 2026, at 6:50 PM PHT in Camiguin Island, Cagayan. The storm brought wind speeds of up to 185 km/h and heavy rainfall, prompting warnings for extreme nor...

item.title: Key Stocks to Watch: Intel, SAP, Oracle, and Volkswagen on July 24, 2026
item.description: The Wall Street Journal's live coverage highlights critical stock market updates for July 24, 2026, focusing on Intel, SAP, Oracle, and Volkswagen. Analysts note Intel's potential shift toward AI-driv...

item.title: Family tragedy as boat capsizes during church trip in Moshi, Tanzania
item.description: Five family members, including two women and three children, drowned when a boat they were traveling in capsized in Nyumba ya Mungu Dam, Moshi, Tanzania. The incident occurred on July 23, 2026, as mem...

item.title: Blessing CEO Granted Bail in ₦69m Fraud Case, Travel Restricted
item.description: A Special Offences Court in Lagos State granted social media personality Blessing Okoro (Blessing CEO) bail of ₦20 million over an alleged ₦69.15 million property fraud. The court imposed conditions i...

item.title: KoRn's Jonathan Davis Weds Brittany Parisi in California Ceremony
item.description: KoRn's frontman Jonathan Davis married his partner Brittany Parisi on July 3, 2026, at the Neighborhood Church of Palos Verdes in California. The wedding ceremony drew inspiration from Sandro Botticel...

item.title: Marcos Jr. launches specialized pediatric center in Clark to improve child healthcare
item.description: President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. recently inaugurated the Jose B. Lingad Children's Medical Center in Clark, Pampanga, a state-of-the-art facility dedicated to providing advanced pediatric care. The eve...

item.title: Alberta's proposed southern pipeline faces seismic, environmental and Indigenous challenges
item.description: Alberta has proposed a new pipeline route through southern British Columbia to deliver oil to the West Coast, but the plan faces significant hurdles. The project would deviate from the existing Trans ...

item.title: Ramaphosa's Legal Battle Against Impeachment Committee Continues
item.description: President Cyril Ramaphosa faces a critical legal battle as the Western Cape High Court prepares to rule on his urgent application to suspend the section 89 impeachment committee investigating the Phal...

item.title: Stevie Wonder Announces New Album with Lost Classic Tracks
item.description: Stevie Wonder, the legendary singer-songwriter, is set to release his first studio album in over two decades titled 'Through The Eyes Of Wonder'. The project, scheduled for 2027, marks a significant m...

item.title: Group Calls on Ekiti Govt to Respect Alara Selection Process
item.description: The Oduduwa Citizen Liberation Vanguard (OCLV) has warned the Ekiti State Government against interfering with the traditional chieftaincy selection process for the new Alara of Aramoko-Ekiti. The grou...

item.title: Intel Demonstrates Strong Recovery and Growth Momentum
item.description: Intel's recent performance has signaled a significant turnaround, demonstrating strong recovery and growth momentum in the semiconductor industry. The company's strategic shifts, including increased i...

item.title: The Quiet Beauty of Fog and Moss in the Pacific Northwest
item.description: The article explores the cultural and natural significance of fog in the Pacific Northwest, where it is not merely a weather phenomenon but a defining mood of the region. It describes how fog transfor...

item.title: CJI Clarifies No Plea Filed on Police Action During CJP Protests
item.description: Chief Justice of India (CJI) Surya Kant clarified that no petition seeking the Supreme Court’s intervention into alleged police excesses during the July 20 crackdown on student protesters in Delhi had...
